name: Cercavins Route – hiking in Catalonia
description:
The landscape of the Segarra region is strongly marked by its dry crops. It is a mosaic of cereal fields, almond trees and vines. The latter make up part of the Costers del Segre designation of origin. The Cercavins Route follows the course of the river of the same name and visits towns such as l'Ametlla de Segarra and Montornès de Segarra. These small nuclei of accentuated rural character hide an interesting cultural and historical heritage that you do not want to miss.

The Route starts from l'Ametlla de Segarra, where you can visit the tower of the old castle, from the 11th century, and also the church of Sant Pere, of which its square bell tower with four eyes topped by a pinnacle stands out. You leave the town towards the south, towards Montoliu de Segarra and the Cercavins river, through paths surrounded by fields. The route does not go through Montoliu, but from the road you have a good view of this picturesque town perched on a hill.

Follow the path, parallel to the river, in the direction of Montornès de Segarra. At the entrance to the town, to your right, you will find the town's cemetery, in a modernist style and very peculiar due to its corners topped by pagodas covered in glazed ceramic. Montornès also houses an interesting heritage: the remains of its castle, attached to the parish church, or the old school, now converted into the town's social premises. It is highly recommended to visit the streets of its old town, whose houses preserve the old voussoired portals. After the visit to Montornès, you return to l'Ametlla again, crossing a varied succession of cultivated fields.
region_intro: Hiking around Montoliu De Segarra offers exploration through a landscape characterized by rolling hills and extensive agricultural land. The region, part of the high plateau of La Segarra, features diverse flora within its shrublands and woodlands. The River Cercavins flows through the area, adding a natural water feature to the terrain. Routes often pass through charming villages with a distinct medieval atmosphere, integrating cultural interest with natural surroundings. Best hiking trails around Montoliu De Segarra The most popular hiking route is Cercavins Route – hiking in Catalonia, an 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain and follows the course of the River Cercavins. Another top favourite among local hikers is Valle del Río Corb — circular por Albió, Segura y Vallfogona del Riu Corb, a moderate 9.3 miles (14.9 km) path. This circular trail explores the Corb River valley, passing through several small villages. Local hikers also love the Countryside Around Albió – Albió loop from Albió, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail leading through agricultural land and open vistas, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Hiking around Montoliu De Segarra is defined by rolling hills, agricultural landscapes, and diverse flora, with routes often passing through medieval villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging trails. The routes in Montoliu De Segarra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from 19 reviews. More than 145 hikers have used komoot to explore Montoliu De Segarra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Montoliu De Segarra?
A: There are over 45 hiking trails around Montoliu De Segarra, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes explore the region's rolling hills, agricultural landscapes, and charming medieval villages.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Montoliu De Segarra?
A: The region experiences a Mediterranean climate, making spring and autumn ideal for hiking. During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, perfect for exploring the diverse flora and open vistas without the intense heat of summer or the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking trails in Montoliu De Segarra?
A: Yes, Montoliu De Segarra offers several easy trails suitable for families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31827148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Countryside Around Albió – Albió loop from Albió&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 6 km path leading through agricultural land and open vistas, typically completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. This route is relatively flat and provides a gentle introduction to the area's scenery.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Montoliu De Segarra?
A: Many trails in Montoliu De Segarra are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural surroundings with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock, and to carry water for them. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in the area?
A: Yes, Montoliu De Segarra features several circular routes, allowing hikers to experience diverse scenery without retracing their steps. A notable moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1134485470/valle-del-rio-corb-circular-por-albio-segura-y-vallfogona-del-riu-corb" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Río Corb — circular por Albió, Segura y Vallfogona del Riu Corb&lt;/a&gt;, a 14.9 km path exploring the Corb River valley and passing through small villages.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I expect to see on the hikes?
A: Hiking in Montoliu De Segarra offers views of rolling hills, extensive farmland, and diverse flora within shrublands and woodlands. The River Cercavins adds a natural water feature to the landscape, as seen on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1200082607/cercavins-route-hiking-in-catalonia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cercavins Route – hiking in Catalonia&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5072094" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Albió&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the surrounding countryside.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or interesting places to explore along the trails?
A: The hiking experience in Montoliu De Segarra often integrates cultural elements, with routes passing through charming villages with a distinct medieval atmosphere. You might encounter historical sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4770425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corral del Monjo&lt;/a&gt; or explore settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4696023" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guimerà&lt;/a&gt;, which also features &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8049582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guimerà Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Main Square – Chapel of Sant Esteve loop from Guimerà&lt;/a&gt; is a good option for combining hiking with historical exploration.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Montoliu De Segarra?
A: While specific parking areas for each trail may vary, Montoliu De Segarra and its surrounding villages typically offer public parking options. It's advisable to check the starting points of your chosen routes on komoot for detailed information on nearby parking facilities.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads?
A: Public transport options to Montoliu De Segarra and its immediate trailheads might be limited, as the region is characterized by quiet, rural areas. It's generally recommended to have personal transportation for greater flexibility in accessing various starting points. However, some larger nearby towns may offer bus services, which could connect to the region.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Montoliu De Segarra?
A: The hiking routes in Montoliu De Segarra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from 19 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the tranquility of the agricultural landscapes, and the charm of passing through medieval villages. Many appreciate the well-maintained paths and the opportunity to connect with nature and history.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: While many routes are moderate, the broader Segarra region, which includes Montoliu De Segarra, is part of larger hiking networks like sections of the 'Cavalls del Vent' trail. These networks can offer more challenging, longer-distance options for experienced trekkers looking for greater elevation gains and extended durations. Within the immediate area, you'll find 3 difficult trails among the 46 available, providing more strenuous options.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking routes?
A: Montoliu De Segarra and the surrounding villages, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5440227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segura&lt;/a&gt;, offer local establishments where you can find places to eat or accommodation. These typically include small restaurants, cafes, or rural guesthouses that cater to visitors exploring the region. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19886973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segura loop from Segura&lt;/a&gt; starts and ends in a village where you might find such amenities.
tips: The tower of l'Ametlla de Segarra is not complete. Here you can see remains of the castle tower, the only structure preserved from the fortress. It has its origins in the 11th century, when this area was under the rule of the Counts of Barcelona.

L'Ametlla de Segarra is a town belonging to the municipality of Montoliu de Segarra, in the Segarra region, province of Lleida. It is linked to the castle of the same name, appearing in records at the end of the 11th century.

It is located in an area with a tradition of cereal farming and other rainfed crops such as olive groves and almond trees. You can also find vineyards, particularly organic vineyards that produce Comalats wine, which was the old name of the region.

Of its heritage, the church of Sant Pere and the tower of the old castle stand out.

The parish church of the nucleus of l'Ametlla de Segarra (Montoliu de Segarra) appears for the first time in the records of the bishopric of Vic in the 11th century.

Located within the urban nucleus, it is attached to the old rectory, it has a single nave with a pointed barrel vault. The bell tower, square and with four eyes structured in semicircular arches, has a clock and is topped by a pinnacle.

The Montornès de Segarra cemetery was built at the end of the 19th century by Josep Balcells i Cortada, an Indian who, on his return from America, dedicated part of his fortune to building this cemetery and also the village school.

The cemetery is built in the modernist style, has a square shape and each of its corners is topped by a pagoda covered in glazed ceramic.
